data.entity_name: Taxonomic classification of Luidia sarsii africana, the Legs break easily starfish (table)
data.content: Taxonomic table for Luidia sarsii africana (LucAfr), the Legs break easily starfish. It lists Phylum Echinodermata, Class Asteroidea, Order Paxillosida, Family Luidiidae, Genus Luidia, Species sarsii africana, and Common name. The table formalizes the page-429 species profile, linking the common name to the diagnostic trait that arms break off easily and supporting comparison with Astropecten polyacanthus and Astropecten exilis.

This image is a distribution map for the marine invertebrate species Luidia sarsii africana, showing observation points along the coast of southern Africa. The map includes depth contours and indicates that the data is based solely on observations. It complements textual information about the species' distribution, size, and distinguishing features found on pages 429 and 430 of the 'Field-Guide-to-SA-Offshore-Marine-Invertebrates' document.

This image shows a Luidia africana starfish against a black background, with a scale bar indicating 80 mm. The starfish has five long, flattened, tapering arms, some reddish-brown and others creamy white. This visual aligns with the descriptive text provided, highlighting the species' distinctive arm morphology and coloration, which are key for identification in marine invertebrate field guides.

data.entity_name: Taxonomic classification of Chondraster elattosis, the Pentagon star (table)
data.content: This table gives the taxonomic classification and common name for Chondraster elattosis (ChoEla), the Pentagon star, a South African endemic deep-water starfish. It anchors the surrounding page 430 species account by linking diagnostic morphology—rigid pentagonal disc, pink-orange aboral surface, pale yellow oral surface, up to 230 mm diameter, 400–1000+ m depth—to formal ranks from Phylum Echinodermata through Family Poraniidae.

This image is a distribution map for the marine invertebrate Chondraster elattosis, showing observation points along the southern coast of Africa. It includes bathymetric contour lines and geographical coordinates, indicating the species' presence in specific oceanic depths. The map is part of a field guide to South African offshore marine invertebrates, specifically referencing page 430 of the document.

This image shows a single, inflated, pentagonal marine invertebrate, likely a starfish, with a speckled orange aboral surface. A scale bar indicates a length of 205 mm. The image is presented against a black background and is likely used for species identification in a marine invertebrate field guide, correlating with descriptive text about its appearance, color, and size.

data.entity_name: Orange-speckled starfish with white radial patterns (image)
data.content: This image shows a starfish with a speckled orange aboral surface and white radial patterns, against a black background. A scale bar indicates a size of 190 mm. This visual representation aligns with descriptions of marine invertebrates, likely used for identification in a field guide. The context suggests it may be related to species with distinct aboral surface patterning, potentially including brooding features, and its size is within the range described for certain starfish species.

data.entity_name: Taxonomic classification table for Spoladaster veneris, the Inflated star (table)
data.content: Taxonomic identification table for Spoladaster veneris (SpoBra), the Inflated star, giving its classification from Echinodermata to veneris. It anchors the page 431 field-guide entry, whose surrounding text describes an inflated, pentagonal, papillate orange starfish from South Africa's West and South Coasts at 40–205+ m. The table supports species identification and comparison with Chondraster elattosis/ellatosis, while a possible OCR/context ambiguity involves S. brachyactis.

This image is a distribution map for the marine invertebrate species 'Spoladaster veneris'. It shows two observation points marked by red dots on the southern coast of South Africa. The map includes geographical coordinates, depth contour lines, and indicates that the data is based solely on observations. This visual aids in understanding the geographical range of the species as described in the surrounding text.

data.entity_name: Inflated orange starfish with speckled aboral surface (image)
data.content: This image shows an inflated, pentagonal starfish with a speckled orange aboral surface against a black background. A 75 mm scale bar is included. The image likely depicts a specimen from the 'Field Guide to SA Offshore Marine Invertebrates', possibly illustrating a species described on page 431, which notes an inflated, cushion-like body with numerous papillae on the aboral surface and a speckled brilliant orange color.

data.entity_name: Pentagonal starfish with mottled orange-brown edges (image)
data.content: This image shows a pentagonal starfish with a pale yellow aboral surface and mottled orange-brown edges, presented against a black background. A 75 mm scale bar is included. The starfish appears inflated and cushion-like, consistent with descriptions of marine invertebrates found in the surrounding text, likely for identification purposes in a field guide.

This table provides the formal taxonomic identity of Poraniopsis echinaster, captioned PorEch and commonly called the Spiky cushion star. It places the species within Echinodermata, Asteriodea, Valvatida, Poraniidae, Poraniopsis, and echinaster. In context, it anchors the page 432 field-guide description of a short-armed, spiny sea star and supports comparison with similar species such as Lophaster quadrispinus and Diplopteraster multipes.

This image is a distribution map for the marine invertebrate Poraniopsis echinaster, showing observation points along the West and South Coasts of South Africa. It visually complements the textual description of the species' distribution found in the surrounding context.
